## Improving Paper Writing Formatting and Spacing

# Paper Writing Guidelines

### Purpose
This document outlines the guidelines for improving paper writing formatting and spacing.

## Table of Contents

## Introduction

### Purpose
The purpose of this document is to provide a comprehensive guide on how to improve paper writing formatting and spacing.

- [x] Use a consistent heading structure throughout the document.
- [x] Ensure proper indentation and spacing between paragraphs.
- [x] Use clear and concise language to convey complex ideas.

## Formatting Standards

> [!TIP]
> **Formatting Standards**:
>
> 1. Dates: `yyyy-mm-dd` format (e.g., `2025-10-18`)
> 2. Time: `hh:mm–hh:mm` format (e.g., `09:00–11:30`)
> 3. Auto-generate Table of Contents using [VS Code Markdown All in One](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=yzhang.markdown-all-in-one#table-of-contents)

## Document Structure

### Milestones
Create a master checklist of all projects, their milestones, and individual tasks.

> [!TIP]
> **Key Principles**:
>
> - One project = One study note (installation/integration/user guide)
> - Each task = One section in the study note
> - Task title must match its section header exactly
> - Link completed tasks to their documentation

## Conclusion

The goal of this document is to provide a comprehensive guide on improving paper writing formatting and spacing.


# Improving Paper Writing Formatting and Spacing

> [!TIP]
> **Formatting Standards**:
>
> 1. Dates: `yyyy-mm-dd` format (e.g., `2025-10-18`
> 2. Time: `hh:mm–hh:mm` format (e.g., `09:00–11:30`
> 3. Auto-generate Table of Contents using [VS Code Markdown All in One](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=yzhang.markdown-all-in-one#table-of-contents)

**Document Structure**: Link your study notes with milestones and daily logs as shown below:

![Daily log workflow diagram](./assets/daily-log.png)

### Milestones

**Purpose**: Create a master checklist of all projects, their milestones, and individual tasks.

> [!TIP]
> **Key Principles**:
>
> - One project = One study note (installation/integration/user guide)
> - Each task = One section in the study note
> - Task title must match its section header exactly
> - Link completed tasks to their documentation

**File**: Create `./milestones.md` in your project repository.

**Template**:

```markdown
title
## Table of Contents
<!-- Auto-generated by Markdown All in One -->

### Project Title
#### Milestone 1: <Milestone Name>
- [x] [1.1 Task Title](https://github.com/bmw-ece-ntust/<repo>/blob/<7-digit commit hash>/<study-note>.md#<task-section>)
- [x] [1.2 Task Title](https://github.com/bmw-ece-ntust/<repo>/blob/<7-digit commit hash>/<s
``
